content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates all four VIEW components consistently. Wonder shows up in single, spacious questions ('That dread — what is it made of?') rather than stacked or strategic probes. Impartiality is clear — the coach does not push toward a boundary script or a particular outcome, and notably resists the client's explicit request for 'literally what words to use' in turn 14, instead trusting the deeper material. Empathy is felt rather than performed — no 'that's so valid' filler; instead, accurate reflections ('That's a very old instruction,' 'The words were never really the problem'). Pace consistently slows when the client is hedging or rushing — coach invites 'stay with that,' 'let it be there for a moment,' 'you don't have to explain it away.' Vulnerability is the weakest leg (coach doesn't name own state in first person), but the undefended quality — willingness to let the conversation expand beyond the presenting question, no need to deliver a tidy answer — substitutes well. No agenda underneath the questions; the questions emerge from what's actually in front of the coach.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Single, open, wondering question. No rush to solve the stated 'how do I set a boundary' problem."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic caveat with curiosity, not interpretation. Leaves space."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Stay with that' — pace slows when client is hedging. Empathic without being saccharine."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Invites the tiredness to just be true. Resists the client's reflexive 'but she's not a villain' without arguing with it. Impartial and gentle."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal, undefended echo. Trusts the client to find it; no agenda to fill the space."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names 'a very old instruction' — accurate, not preachy. Reframes without prescribing. Wonder for the actual phenomenon."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Beautiful non-defensive response to client's pull back to 'just give me words.' Coach stays with what is actually alive rather than chasing the surface request."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "view"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "empowered-vs-power"],
  "notes": "Stance is strong even though several signature moves (love test, what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want) are not deployed — those are Methodology Fidelity concerns, not stance. Coach could have brought more first-person vulnerability ('I notice...') to be a textbook 3, but the consistent undefended, non-agenda quality earns it."
}
